Class LabeledTermImpl

java.lang.Object
de.uka.ilkd.key.logic.LabeledTermImpl
All Implemented Interfaces:
EqualsModProperty<JTerm>, JTerm, Sorted, LogicElement, Sorted, SyntaxElement, Term
public class LabeledTermImpl extends Object

The labeled term class is used for terms that have a label attached.

Two labeled terms are equal if they have equal term structure and equal annotations. In contrast, the method EqualsModProperty.equalsModProperty(Object, Property, Object[]) can be used to compare terms while ignoring certain given properties. E.g. by using RenamingTermProperty.RENAMING_TERM_PROPERTY, just the term structures modulo renaming are compared whilst ignoring annotations. *
See Also:
  • JTerm
  • TermImpl

    The visitor is handed through till the bottom of the tree, and then it walks upwards, while at each upstep the method visit of the visitor is called.

    The visitor walks downwards the tree, while at each downstep the method visit of the visitor is called.

  • Constructor Details

    • LabeledTermImpl

      public LabeledTermImpl(Operator op, ImmutableArray<JTerm> subs, ImmutableArray<QuantifiableVariable> boundVars, ImmutableArray<TermLabel> labels, String origin)
      creates an instance of a labeled term.
      Parameters:
      op - the top level operator
      subs - the Term that are the subterms of this term
      boundVars - logic variables bound by the operator
      labels - the term's labels (must not be null or empty)
      origin - a String with origin information

    • LabeledTermImpl

      public LabeledTermImpl(Operator op, ImmutableArray<JTerm> subs, ImmutableArray<QuantifiableVariable> boundVars, ImmutableArray<TermLabel> labels)
      creates an instance of a labeled term.
      Parameters:
      op - the top level operator
      subs - the Term that are the subterms of this term
      boundVars - logic variables bound by the operator
      labels - the terms labels (must not be null or empty)

      public @Nullable String getOrigin()
      Description copied from interface: JTerm
      Returns a human-readable source of this term. For example the filename with line and offset.
      Specified by:
      getOrigin in interface JTerm

    • checked

      public JTerm checked()
      Checks whether the Term is valid on the top level. If this is the case this method returns the Term unmodified. Otherwise, a TermCreationException is thrown.

    • equalsModProperty

      public <V> boolean equalsModProperty(Object o, Property<JTerm> property, V... v)
      Description copied from interface: EqualsModProperty
      Checks whether this object is equal to o modulo the property described by property.

      Some properties call for additional arguments. For more information on that, see the documentation of equalsModThisProperty for the certain property.

      To get an overview of the currently implemented properties, see the documentation of EqualsModProperty.

      Specified by:
      equalsModProperty in interface EqualsModProperty<JTerm>
      Type Parameters:
      V - the type of the additional parameters needed by property for the comparison
      Parameters:
      o - the object that is checked for equality
      property - the property to be ignored in the equality check
      v - the additional arguments needed by property for the equality check
      Returns:
      whether this object is equal to o

      public int hashCodeModProperty(Property<? super JTerm> property)

      Computes the hash code according to the given ignored property.
      Specified by:
      hashCodeModProperty in interface EqualsModProperty<JTerm>
      Parameters:
      property - the ignored property according to which the hash code is computed
      Returns:
      the hash code of this object
